procedure:
Neuromodulator (NeuroMod)
Location: Face
Neuromodulator: 30 units
Lot# C8093AC4
Expiration date: 10/2025
Discussed all common benefits, including improvement in skin lines by paralyzing certain groups of muscles which may lasts from 3-7 months and risks associated with the procedure which includes but is not limited to swelling, rash, headache, local numbness, pain at injection site, bruising, ptosis, drooping of eyelids, and asymmetry of facial lines.Although rare, the patient was informed that infection, respiratory problems and allergic reactions may occur. The patient denies any history of allergies to botulinum toxin or any of its ingredients, denies history of Guillaine Barre syndrome or any neurological problems. Prior to starting, skin was prepared with alcohol. Using a syringe, botulinum toxin was injected to the indicated areas. Patient tolerated the procedure well. Advised patient to avoid laying flat for the next 2 hours.
Filler (Filler)
Location: Lips
Quantity: 1
Lot# 1000535086
Expiration date: 07/17/2024
Quantity injected: 0.7ml
Discussed benefits of procedure including improvement in wrinkles, lines, folds in the face, which may last for approximately 4-9 months, alternatives such as not doing any treatment, other forms of fillers, and risks of the procedure, which includes but is not limited to swelling, pain, itching, discoloration, bruising, asymmetry, and tenderness at the injection site. The patient was made aware that there may be increased bruising or discoloration with concurrent use of blood thinners, such as aspirin or other non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs such as Advil. The very rare risks of skin necrosis, granuloma, and blindness, especially if used around the eyes, nose, or frown lines. The development of visible lumps or nodules was discussed. The patient denied any history of allergies or hypersensitivity to components of fillers. The patient agreed to have the filler injection. Prior to the procedure, written informed consent was signed by the patient. After cleaning the area with alcohol, filler was injected. The patient tolerated the procedure well. Ice packs were placed on the area to prevent bruising. The patient was informed to call us for any signs of infection which includes redness, warmth, formation of lumps, and tender areas. The patient understood the instructions.

patientSummary: 39 years old Female patient, Evely Avalos was seen on 04-29-2025 as a follow up patient.

complaints: Patient is interested in botox and filler.
assesment:
Aesthetics - Wrinkles
 Botox done today. 
Discussed with patient that Botox is a type of neuromodulator that causes temporary paralyzation of relaxation of the muscle to reduce the formation of wrinkles. Patient aware that results take around 2 weeks to take full effect. Results are not permanent and will need re-treatment. Patient aware of R/B/SE/A of neuromodulator treatment. Patient aware that adverse effects can cause headache, bruising, pain, twitching, asymmetry, brow or eyelid ptosis, rash, inability to frown and raise eyebrows, and difficulty swallowing if the neck is treated.
